configurations.name: with_skill
configurations.description: Using the human-voice-writer skill (iteration 2, with negation-assertion ban)

evals.prompt: Write a 3-paragraph blog post about why remote work is here to stay. Target audience is HR professionals.

expectations.text: Output contains none of the Tier 1 banned AI vocabulary
Output contains no em dashes, en dashes, or double hyphens
Sentence length standard deviation is 7 or more words
Output has exactly 3 paragraphs as requested
Output does not open with a banned opening

Sentence length standard deviation is 7 or more words
Output has exactly 3 paragraphs as requested
Output does not open with a banned opening
No em dashes, en dashes, or double hyphens
No em dashes, en dashes, or double hyphens

evals.prompt: Write a Slack message about database migration completion

expectations.text: No em dashes, en dashes, or double hyphens
No em dashes, en dashes, or double hyphens

comparison_with_iteration_1.improvement: with_skill went from 94% to 100% pass rate (+6%), fixing the negation-assertion and burstiness issues from iteration 1

analyst_notes: with_skill achieved 100% pass rate across all 3 evals (20/20 assertions), up from 94% in iteration 1
The negation-assertion ban added in iteration 2 was successfully followed by the with_skill run (blog post passed the new check)
Burstiness improved significantly: SD=11.1 in iteration 2 vs 7.4 in iteration 1 (threshold lowered from 8.0 to 7.0 but would have passed at 8.0 too)
Em dashes remain the most consistent baseline failure: without_skill failed in all 3 evals (blog: --, de-ai: --, slack: em dash)
without_skill baseline pass rate increased from 61% to 70%, suggesting some run-to-run variance in baseline outputs
The negation-assertion check is non-discriminating in this run: both with_skill and without_skill passed it for the blog post. More test cases needed to validate this assertion's discriminating power
Token overhead of skill is consistent: ~32% more tokens (44k vs 34k avg), similar to iteration 1
Time overhead is ~3x (58s vs 20s) due to skill reading and self-review passes
